title: Excel Analytical Tool for GreenComp-based Questionnaire (GCQuest)
author: Sousa, Bruno
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The  GCQuest Analysis Tool  has been designed to enhance the analysis of data collected via the  GreenComp-based Questionnaire (GCQuest) , a versatile instrument tailored for a range of activities and target audiences. This tool streamlines the process by allowing users to input participant data into the "Data Entry" worksheet, where it is automatically organised and categorised by typology—Knowledge, Aptitude, or Attitude—and by dimension—Valuing Sustainability, Supporting Equity, or Promoting Nature—within the broader GreenComp framework. 
 Additionally, the tool generates several graphs that visualise the results, making it easier to incorporate them into presentations or reports on sustainability. While the current instructions and layout are available in English, users can switch the language of the items and scale names using a dropdown menu. The research team welcomes proposals for translations into different languages, which can be discussed via the email address   dep-educity@ua.pt  . Note that the tool's instructions in Excel will remain in English regardless of the chosen language for the results.
steps_raw:
The  GCQuest Analysis Tool  has been designed to enhance the analysis of data collected via the  GreenComp-based Questionnaire (GCQuest) , a versatile instrument tailored for a range of activities and target audiences. This tool streamlines the process by allowing users to input participant data into the "Data Entry" worksheet, where it is automatically organised and categorised by typology—Knowledge, Aptitude, or Attitude—and by dimension—Valuing Sustainability, Supporting Equity, or Promoting Nature—within the broader GreenComp framework. 
 Additionally, the tool generates several graphs that visualise the results, making it easier to incorporate them into presentations or reports on sustainability. While the current instructions and layout are available in English, users can switch the language of the items and scale names using a dropdown menu. The research team welcomes proposals for translations into different languages, which can be discussed via the email address   dep-educity@ua.pt  . Note that the tool's instructions in Excel will remain in English regardless of the chosen language for the results.
